channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Mike Bibby is already bringing in former NBA players' sons as he begins rebuilding Sacramento State
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Mike Bibby and Shaquille O'Neal are going to be mixing it up again, all these years later. No more Kings-Lakers rivalry or on-court trash talk. Now, it's Bibby the college coach and O'Neal the student-athlete father.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NFL postpones a decision on the tush push but passes other rule changes
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — A decision on the future of the tush push has been pushed until next month. NFL team owners had been set to vote Tuesday on Green Bay’s proposal to ban the play that’s helped the Philadelphia Eagles win one Super Bowl and reach another, but it was tabled until May.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: At 89, Fred Costello plays the organ on opening day in Rochester just like he has since 1977
item.description: Fred Costello could swing a bat about as well as he could perform a melody as a kid growing up in Syracuse, New York. He wanted to play at ballparks, and figured the best way to reach that dream was through slick fielding and a sweet swing.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ithaca College lets trans athlete compete in women's rowing competition after NCAA changes gender policy
item.description: Ithaca College has admitted to letting a trans athlete compete on the rowing team, and the NCAA has not indicated that the school will face consequences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LPGA Tour takes center stage in Las Vegas as PGA Tour plots future return
item.description: Reigning Player of the Year and world No. 1 Nelly Korda is back in the Las Vegas area this week to defend her T-Mobile Match Play title.
item.dc:creator: Mark Anderson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Radical ideas for the Pac-12 football schedule to (potentially) boost media rights value and “separate” itself
item.description: If the conference is willing to get creative, it could boost the price of its upcoming media rights contract.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Super Bowl champ Richard Sherman joins long list of sports figures whose homes have been burglarized
item.description: MAPLE VALLEY, Calif. (AP) — Former NFL player Richard Sherman is the latest sports figure whose home has been burglarized. The Super Bowl champion posted pictures and a video on social media asking if anyone recognized three armed intruders that broke into his house last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In recruiting battle, lawmakers weigh whether to give college athletes a tax break on NIL earnings
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Illinois state Rep. Travis Weaver remembers how surreal it felt having Heisman Trophy winner Mark Ingram sit a few rows behind him in a political science class. He will never forget the pure joy in Alabama every time the Crimson Tide rolled to the national championship, either.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Red Sox, Garrett Crochet agree to 6-year, $170 million contract extension: report
item.description: The Boston Red Sox and starting pitcher Garrett Crochet reportedly agreed to a six-year, $170 million contract after the team acquired Crochet in the offseason.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Yankee player's son may have died of carbon monoxide poisoning
item.description: Miller Gardner, the 14-year-old son of former Yankees outfielder Brett Gardner, may have died from abnormal levels of carbon monoxide.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Auburn cheerleaders send college basketball fans swooning
item.description: The Auburn Tigers cheerleading team went viral on Monday after the men's basketball team defeated Michigan State in the Elite Eight of the tournament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Caitlin Clark being 'White girl from the middle of America' helped connect with young fans, ESPN pundit says
item.description: ESPN basketball analyst Monica McNutt suggested Caitlin Clark being a "White girl from the middle of America" played into her connection with young girls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes 1st Cubs player to hit for the cycle in 32 years in 18-3 rout of A's in West Sacramento
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Carson Kelly had come close to hitting for the cycle, yet that elusive triple kept holding him back. He had just two triples over a 10-year big league career before Monday night. That's when Kelly became the first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thunder rout Bulls 145-117 for 10th straight win, improve to 28-1 vs the East
item.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 27 points and 12 assists in just 27 minutes, and the Oklahoma City Thunder rolled to a 145-117 victory over the Chicago Bulls on Monday night for their 10th straight victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Garrett Crochet agrees to $170 million, 6-year deal with Red Sox
item.description: Ace left-hander Garrett Crochet has agreed to a record-breaking $170 million, six-year contract with the Boston Red Sox.
item.dc:creator: Jay Cohen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zion Williamson's season is over, as Pelicans shut down the forward and CJ McCollum
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Zion Williamson and CJ McCollum will miss the rest of the season because of injuries, the New Orleans Pelicans said Monday. Williamson sustained a low back bone contusion after a fall during a game on March 19 in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen Collins makes stance on trans athletes in women's sports clear as Maine bucks Trump's executive order
item.description: Maine Sen. Susan Collins said in a statement that she doesn't believe "transgender athletes should compete in girls’ and women’s athletics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kanon Catchings enters transfer portal
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After a disappointing freshman season, BYU forward Kanon Catchings has entered the transfer portal. The highly-touted 4-star recruit came to BYU with high expectations. But injuries and lack of playing time derailed his freshman season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Padres sweep Braves, 4-0 to begin a season for the first time since 1984
item.description: Break out the broomsticks! The San Diego Padres sweep the Atlanta Braves to begin their 2025 MLB season.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Moncada and Soriano deliver as the Angels edge the White Sox 1-0
item.description: Yoán Moncada hit an RBI single against his former team in the eighth inning, and the Los Angeles Angels blanked the Chicago White Sox 1-0 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz get blown out by Denver, 129-93
item.description: DENVER (ABC4 Sports) - One more loss by the Utah Jazz will assure the team of its worst record in franchise history, dating all the way back to 1974 in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: CMU men's lacrosse escapes Concorida as Maverick women dominate CSU Pueblo
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Colorado Mesa's Men's Lacrosse team looked in full control on their way to a sixth win when they held a 7-3 second-half lead over RMAC foe Concordia University Irvine. Then came a Golden Eagle onslaught. Three quick goals and CUI was within one.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Bulls face the Mavericks on 4-game win streak
item.description: Dallas Mavericks (36-38, 10th in the Western Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (33-40, ninth in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: Chicago aims to keep its four-game win streak intact when the Bulls take on Dallas. The Bulls are 14-22 in home games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Miguel Amaya has 2 doubles and 5 RBIs to lead the Cubs past the Diamondbacks 10-6
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Miguel Amaya hit two doubles and had five RBIs, Ian Happ added a homer, double and three RBIs and the Chicago Cubs beat the Arizona Diamondbacks 10-6 on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NFL legend Randy White fights fentanyl after personal tragedy
item.description: Jesse Dempsey, a professional snowboarder, unknowingly took a pill laced with fentanyl, believing it was a prescribed painkiller for a knee injury.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dorman, NewsNation

item.title: Jazz lose for 14th time in last 15 games, 121-110
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Alperen Sengun had 33 points and 10 rebounds, Amen Thompson finished with 16 points, 10 rebounds and five assists, and the Houston Rockets beat the Utah Jazz 121-110 on Thursday night. The Jazz have now lost 14 of its last 15 games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Sox fans shower Derrick Rose with MVP chants during first pitch
item.description: CHICAGO - Coming back to Chicago never gets old for Derrick Rose. The former Bulls MVP returned to the South Side Thursday to throw out the first pitch at the White Sox home opener, where he was showered with MVP chants from the crowd. "It's surreal. It's always surreal.
item.dc:creator: Rick Tarsitano, WGN 9

item.title: Padres rally in seventh inning to win home opener against Braves
item.description: The Padres kicked off the 2025-2026 season with a comeback victory over the Atlanta Braves Thursday at Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: White Sox hammer Angels in lopsided Opening Day win
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The weather on White Sox Opening Day mirrored their franchise outlook heading into 2025. Murky, gray and dreary with little to be excited about besides the fact baseball season is all the way back. Good thing the White Sox played well in spite of the melancholy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: SF's Chase Center hosting NCAA Men's Basketball games Thursday, Saturday
item.description: (BCN) -- San Francisco is hosting two games at Chase Center on Thursday in the NCAA Men's Basketball Championship tournament. At 4:39 p.m., University of Florida, which is the No. 1 seed in the West region of the tournament's bracket, plays No. 4 University of Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: MTS boosts Trolley service for Padres Opening Weekend
item.description: The Friar Faithful will be taking over downtown San Diego on Thursday and into the weekend as the Padres take on the Atlanta Braves, marking the first home games of the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Ed Lenderman, Fox 5

item.title: Loyola Chicago men's basketball team beats Kent State, advances to NIT semifinals
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Loyola Chicago men's basketball team didn't make the 68-team NCAA Tournament field this season, but the Ramblers are still ramblin' on to a different Final Four.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: NCAA Tournament regionals: A look at Chicagoland connections, program history and more for the men's basketball Sweet 16
item.description: The 2025 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament resumes Thursday night, and it's down to the Sweet 16. It'll be the Elite Eight after the regional semifinals on Thursday and Friday and then the Final Four after the regional finals on Saturday and Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Florida coach brings team to famed local staple ahead of March Madness matchup in SF
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Florida head coach Todd Golden is excited to be back in San Francisco this week -- a city where he began his head coaching career. Golden leads the No. 1 seed Gators for their Sweet 16 matchup Thursday against No. 4 seed Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Rays prepare for Tampa home opener
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The Tampa Bay Rays have their home opener for their season at Steinbrenner Field on Friday. The game is already a sell out. Seating capacity at this years games will be greatly reduced, due to major hurricane damage at Tropicana Field.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Softball: Poway 2, Patrick Henry 0
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- A gorgeous day to play softball on Monday afternoon with Patrick Henry hosting Poway. The Titans won a fantastic game 2-0 in 10 innings. Great pitching on both sides with Mya McGowan leading the way for Poway with a complete game shutout and adding 12 strikeouts.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: This California MLB team has highest median ticket price, new report finds
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5
